channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: The Midwest golf company that's changing the game in Sycamore, Illinois
item.description: Sub 70, a golf company based in Sycamore, Illinois, offers custom-made golf equipment and personal service to its customers, while maintaining its Midwest roots and high-quality craftsmanship.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: Alex Bregman and Red Sox agree to $120 million, 3-year contract, AP source says
item.description: Alex Bregman and the Boston Red Sox have agreed to a $120 million, three-year contract, a person familiar with the deal tells The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ronald Blum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Briscoe gives Toyota its first Daytona 500 pole as Johnson and Truex also lock into NASCAR opener
item.description: Chase Briscoe led a banner night for Toyota in qualifying for the Daytona 500 by winning the pole for NASCAR’s “Great American Race.”.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Fryer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bengals' Joe Burrow says he put up MVP-caliber season despite lingering wrist injury
item.description: Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow said during a recent appearance on Barstool Sports' "Pardon My Take" that his previous wrist injury still bothered him at times during his recent MVP-caliber season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Egor Demin leads BYU past West Virginia, 73-69
item.description: MORGANTOWN, W.V. (ABC4 Sports) - Freshman Egor Demin scored 16 points to lead BYU to a 73-69 win over West Virginia on Tuesday night. Kanon Catchings added 11 points for BYU (16-8, 7-6 Big 12). "Gutsy win by our guys," said BYU head coach Kevin Young.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago State begins search for head football coach
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The South Side's Chicago State University launched a search to find their first head football coach Tuesday. “We are excited to reach this milestone in our football planning.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Avery Neff makes stunning recovery from ankle injury
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Back January 17th, the #1 gymnastics recruit in the country, freshman Avery Neff was sailing along in her floor routine until her ankle buckled. The Huntsman Center went quiet as Neff had to be carried off the mat. Even she thought her season was over.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Clayton Kershaw to return to Los Angeles Dodgers: reports
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers legend Clayton Kershaw is returning to the defending World Series champions for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Thoughts on the Chicago Bears from around the NFL on Radio Row during Super Bowl week
item.description: Chicago Bears new head coach Ben Johnson is expected to bring a fresh perspective to the team, while quarterback Caleb Williams is expected to elevate his game with the help of Johnson's expertise.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Super Bowl returning to the Bay Area for 2026
item.description: (KRON) -- The Super Bowl is coming back to the Bay Area next year. A decade ago, the region hosted Super Bowl 50. Next year, it will play host to Super Bowl LX. On Monday morning, San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan was in New Orleans for the official Super Bowl handoff.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Ex-MLB player Jacque Jones has high school jersey retired
item.description: Jacque Jones, a former Major League Baseball outfielder and San Diego native, had his legacy cemented in his alma mater's history on Saturday with the official retirement of his jersey number.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Super Bowl LIX highlights: Memorable plays, moving tributes and a historic presidential visit
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 40-22 in the first Super Bowl to be held in New Orleans in a dozen years. It was played amid heavy security — and with President Donald Trump in attendance — just 41 days after a man plowed a truck into New Year's revelers on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, Fox 5

item.title: Former Bears coach Dick Jauron dies at 74
item.description: Longtime NFL player and coach Dick Jauron, who led the Chicago Bears to the playoffs and was voted AP coach of the year in 2001, died Saturday. He was 74.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Vucevic and the Bulls take on Curry and the Warriors
item.description: Golden State Warriors (25-25, 11th in the Western Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (22-30, 10th in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Saturday, 8 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Bulls host the Golden State Warriors in out-of-conference play on Saturday. The Bulls are 10-16 in home games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Martinez scores 30 as USU beats Fresno State, 89-81
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - Ian Martinez scored 30 points and led Utah State to an 89-81 victory over Fresno State Friday night. Martinez, who made 10 of 14 shots from the field, tied a career high with eight rebounds for the Aggies (21-3, 11-2 Mountain West Conference).
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: House Divided: Wife an Eagles fan, husband cheers for Chiefs in NOLA
item.description: When one of the couple grows up in Kansas a Chiefs fan, and the other in South Jersey an Eagles fan, one would expect the marriage to be tested but Jaime and Dave Dailey make it fun.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, Fox 5

item.title: Shaun King and the Bucs’ Super Bowl XXXVII Victory: A Different View
item.description: Shaun King was at Qualcomm Stadium in San Diego, watching his team, the Tampa Bay Buccaneers, dominate Super Bowl XXXVII. It was surreal. Just a few seasons ago, he had been under center, leading this franchise through tough playoff battles.
item.dc:creator: Rod Carter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Public visitation scheduled for Virginia Halas McCaskey
item.description: Virginia Halas McCaskey, the 102-year-old matriarch of the Chicago Bears, will be remembered on Tuesday, Feb. 11, at an event held by Oehler Funeral Home in Des Plaines.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: How Josh Allen beat out Lamar Jackson for AP NFL MVP
item.description: For the past 11 seasons, being named the AP first-team All-Pro quarterback was a prelude to winning the NFL’s MVP award.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dubow, Associated Press, Local 4
